frame_analyses.description: Title card 'Ligature training' with DR-Med branding visible. Penrose drain and instruments in view.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational setup phase; no active task execution yet.

frame_analyses.description: Right needle driver (upper right) and left grasper (lower left) positioned. Needle visible but not yet loaded.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Instruments properly arranged in view. Penrose drain visible with orange stripe markers.

frame_analyses.description: Right needle driver grasping needle at approximately 2/3 point from tip. Needle oriented for entry.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good needle grip position; needle orientation appears perpendicular to drain.

frame_analyses.description: Right needle driver advancing needle toward first mark on Penrose drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth approach; needle trajectory appears aligned with marked entry point.

frame_analyses.description: Needle entering drain at marked line; suture thread visible trailing behind needle.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Entry point appears on-target. Thread control maintained.

frame_analyses.description: Needle exiting opposite side of drain at second marked point. Both entry and exit visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean passage through drain. Exit appears aligned with second mark.

frame_analyses.description: Suture now passed through drain. Both needle ends and long tail visible outside trainer.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Successful suture placement. Adequate length on both sides for knot tying.

frame_analyses.description: Both graspers approaching suture ends to initiate knot formation. Instruments in position.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Right grasper (upper) and left grasper (lower) both grasping suture strands for first throw.

frame_analyses.description: Knot forming; both strands crossed and instruments manipulating into first throw configuration.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hands switching position for knot twist. First throw formation in progress.

frame_analyses.description: First throw complete; knot visible at drain entry point with appropriate tension.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Tight, secure first throw. Knot positioned at or very near marked line.

frame_analyses.description: Graspers repositioning; preparing to introduce knot pusher. Suture tails still visible and separate.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Instruments clearing field for pusher introduction. Knot remains secure.

frame_analyses.description: Knot pusher (gray instrument, upper right) advancing toward knot on drain. Suture tails controlled.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth pusher approach. Drain stable. Tails held taut for proper cinching.

frame_analyses.description: Knot pusher at knot; beginning to advance knot down suture strand onto drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good pusher positioning. Knot cinching in progress with controlled technique.

frame_analyses.description: Knot advanced partway down; both suture tails visible but being pulled tight by graspers.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Progressive cinching. Tension maintained. Knot staying at marked region.

frame_analyses.description: Scissors (small instrument, right side) positioning near suture tail to cut excess suture.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Cut location appropriate; knot already set on drain.

penalties.description: No observable deviations from marks, no gap visible in drain, knot secure and properly positioned, no drain avulsion.

suture_placement.notes: Both entry and exit points align very closely with marked lines. Minimal deviation.

knot_assessment.notes: Clean, tight first throw with knot centered at marked ligature site.

confidence_rationale: This is an instructional/demonstration video with clear teaching intent (DR-Med branding, 'Ligature training' title card). While technique execution is observable and appears expert-level, the video does not show a complete formal FLS attempt with timing mechanism. Suture placement deviations are minimal (≤0.5mm) based on visible alignment with orange stripe markers. Knot quality and drain integrity are clearly excellent. However, the final suture cut phase is not fully shown, and no official timer or formal test conditions are evident. Confidence is moderated due to instructional context rather than formal assessment context.
cannot_determine: Exact mm deviation at each mark — camera angle is slightly oblique, so measurements are estimates based on visual alignment with orange stripe markers
Whether second or third throws occur — video appears to end after first throw demonstration
Formal completion time — no timer visible; estimated from frame progression

demonstrated_techniques: Proper needle driver grip at 2/3 point from needle tip
Perpendicular needle entry and exit for clean suture passage
Extracorporeal knot formation with hand switching
Knot pusher introduction and controlled cinching
Smooth instrument coordination and field management
verbal_cues: Title indicates 'Ligature training' — teaching focus on ligature/knot placement
common_errors_addressed: Avoiding oblique needle entry by maintaining perpendicular trajectory
Preventing suture deviation by aligning with marked lines
Ensuring knot tightness and position at marked site before pushing

technique_summary: Expert-level instructional demonstration of extracorporeal suture and knot tying on a Penrose drain. Needle placement is precise, knot formation is clean and tight, and drain integrity is maintained throughout. All observable technique elements meet or exceed FLS standards.
strengths: Perpendicular suture passage with minimal deviation from marks
Clean, secure first throw with proper tension
Smooth knot pusher introduction and cinching technique
Clear instructional framing with visible markers and proper setup
instructional_assessment: This is an official DR-Med educational resource demonstrating expert technique for Task 4 (Extracorporeal Suture). The video is well-suited for trainee review of proper instrument handling, needle placement precision, and knot formation mechanics. No formal score should be applied; this is a teaching tool, not a scored performance.
